evals.prompt: yo can you tell me if my code-reviewer skill works in cursor or codex? i'm pretty sure cursor uses .mdc files but idk what the rules are. it's at skills/code-reviewer/. give me a real answer not a guess
evals.expected_output: Compatibility matrix covering every provider returned by `providers.mjs` (currently Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex CLI, Agents-Skills Baseline). For each provider: verdict (compatible / partial / incompatible) plus specific reasons grounded in the skill's actual frontmatter and body — not vibes. Should explicitly note that Cursor uses .mdc rule files in .cursor/rules/ rather than SKILL.md, and that Codex uses AGENTS.md. A COMPAT.md file should also be written next to the skill or in the cwd.

assertions.text: covers the Agents-Skills baseline (or a baseline-compatible consumer)

evals.prompt:
i pulled this from a teammate, they said it's a skill but it's got `globs:` and `alwaysApply:` in the frontmatter. will it load in claude code or codex? here's the file:

---
description: Vue 3 patterns
globs: ['**/*.vue']
alwaysApply: false
---

# Vue 3 patterns

Use the composition API. Prefer `<script setup>`. Avoid Options API.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the file as a Cursor .mdc rule, not a SKILL.md. Notes that `globs` and `alwaysApply` are Cursor-specific fields and the file lacks the `name` field required by Claude Code and the `skills` CLI. Verdict: incompatible for Claude Code, incompatible for Codex (which expects AGENTS.md format), native to Cursor. Calls out what would need to change to make it portable (add `name`, drop Cursor-specific fields or move them under a namespaced key).

assertions.text: gives a not-compatible verdict for Claude Code (missing required `name` field is a hard reject, not partial)

evals.prompt: audit skills/ts-best-practices-functional/ — it references AskUserQuestion and TaskCreate in the body. does this break compat with codex or the baseline (gemini / opencode)? i need specifics
evals.expected_output: Identifies AskUserQuestion and TaskCreate as Claude Code-specific tool names. Notes Codex CLI and the Agents-Skills baseline consumers (Gemini CLI uses snake_case run_shell_command etc., OpenCode uses lowercase bash etc.) do not expose those exact tool names — agents in those providers won't be able to call them by name. Recommends either (a) phrasing the body in terms of capabilities ('ask the user a structured question') so any provider's tool surface can fulfill it, or (b) gating the tool-specific instructions behind a 'when running in Claude Code' note. Verdict: portable in spirit, partially compatible in letter.

assertions.text: calls out the task-tracking tool (TaskCreate, the modern Claude Code name) as Claude-specific

evals.prompt: go through all my public skills and tell me which ones are claude-only vs portable. just give me the matrix
evals.expected_output: Discovers skills under skills/*/SKILL.md without being told the path. Parallelizes the audit (one agent per provider, or one per skill, but not strictly serial). Produces a single matrix with rows = skills, columns = providers, cells = compat verdict. Writes the matrix to a file too. Should NOT just pick one skill — should cover all of them.

assertions.text: matrix has at least 3 distinct skill rows (one verdict per row)
produces a matrix that names every provider returned by providers.mjs

evals.prompt: is ts-best-practices cross-provider safe? quick yes/no with reasons
evals.expected_output: Recognition test — ts-best-practices has portable frontmatter (name + description, no Claude-specific tool names in body). Verdict should be 'compatible' or 'mostly compatible' across all providers, with a note that Cursor would need the file renamed to .cursor/rules/ts-best-practices.mdc but the content travels. Should NOT false-positive by inventing problems that don't exist.

assertions.text: gives a positive compat verdict overall (must NOT match `incompatible` — uses negative lookbehind)
actually inspected the frontmatter (cites name or description)
